reviews.review_msg: She is a boring person because of the way she speaks in lectures. But stats is a boring subject. I will take her other classes if offered, if there are no other options.

The professor only read ppt, but not explain the concept.
She not even speaks English fluently.

This is for stat 270 online, Professor Wang was reasonable and gave us fair exams. She also provided us extra chapter problems which were extremely helpful. Her lectures are quite boring but they are clear and her notes are organized.

Dr Wang is a nice and caring person. Lectures were pretty dry. Make sure you know how to derive formulae -- that was a huge focus in all the exams. The exams were pretty tight on time, with barely enough thinking time. The notes are very organized though, which is a big plus. The textbook is very helpful, as is the TA.

Exams resembled lecture content but barely reflected assignments. Assignments involved computational problems, while most exam questions were proofs. Ensure you can derive everything (not exaggerating). The exams are fair but are difficult to finish on time. The TA and the textbook were essential to my success in the course.

Dr. Wang is a nice person but lecture is kind of boring. She gave 5% bonus as long as you finish the participation quiz. She is very happy to help if you have questions. Midterms are closely related to samples. This course is hard in nature, and extremely focus on math. Final exam is very difficult, be prepared to study hard to get a good grade.

The course structure wasn't great. The first 10 weeks covered trivial concepts at an extremely slow pace and the last three weeks were crammed with way more complex concepts. There are minimal practice questions available. The final was terribly structured, only testing maybe 10% of what we learned in the semester.

For a stats course that is very heavy on the math, she ain't the worst prof to have. The textbook isn't really worth reading, just follow her provided lecture notes. Though I do have to say, the final was absurd in difficulty compared to everything else in the course..
Amazing professor. Cares about student's success.
Prof Wang is super caring and helpful. The class is relatively light and easy for someone with a statistics background. If you ever get stuck, she will always help you during her office hours.
She is a nice person. But a bad prof. The material is not hard, grading is kinda harsh. She does not respond to email, this is what pisses me off.
Reads from a PDF, no context or conceptual learning
good prof
Every lecture seemed put together the day before or from another course. We barely covered any material and what we did didn't seem relevant. The notes were excellent (much better than the lectures).
One of the worst prof I had. Her lecture is boring and dry, and she literally just reads her slides during class. Has to teach myself through out the semester.

Liangliang is a good prof. She is kind, patient, ethical and does her best to explain concepts. Sometimes she fails to explain the material clearly or does not provide intuitive explanations. But generally, she is a good teacher. Her midterms are of average difficulty. If you work diligently an A is guaranteed.

Had her for 330 as well and she was solid for both. Final was very difficult but the rest of the course was fairly straightforward. A good introduction to the topics of multivariate analysis. A good choice here.

Her exams are very straightforward if you manage to understand her lecture notes and examples.  If not, the textbook is quite helpful as well.  Midterms were both really easy, final was a bit more difficult (but mainly due to its length and not the questions themselves).  Moderately easy to do well; finished with an A+

Liangliang is a very nice prof, she is a bit hard to understand at first, but I think people have to remember, English is her second language. The biggest problem for me is the fact that she reads of her slides instead of showing how to do calculate the problems.

Prof Liang is very kind to her students. Her tests are fair, just do the practice exams, past hws for review, and read some parts of the textbook and you'll do fine. She uploads slides with her notes which is awesome.
The best prof I ever have

Liang is a new prof+she's NICE. However, she goes really slow in class+repeats herself in lecture to be CLEAR. Most students complain about not being able to understand her but trust me, she speaks fine. I mean she attended grad school in McGill.  She's kind in that she provides practice exams. No need for text, just show up and understand slides.
it's a easy course!!! take it. Dr wang perhaps is not the best prof in stat, but she is super kind. the practice midterm and final are very useful.
she is very kind and hard  working. but the final is very hard. i do understand that this is her first semester in SFU but she really need improve her teaching skill.

Liangliang is a good prof though her expression is not that clear. Her midterms are pretty easy and straightfoward. As long as you do the practice questions, you will do well in the exam. And the average of this course is high.

It is her first time ever teaching a class in a university this semester. She is very unclear when she explains, very boring, very dry. You pretty much teach everything to yourself, she reads off her PowerPoint slides and does not give many examples at all, just lists rules for formulas. Barely anyone attends her lectures, worst prof I've ever had.
